description: The canonical repo was committed (git commit ran and succeeded) BEFORE any --apply, --adopt-new-skills, or --apply-stale flag was executed on the propagate command.
The --classify output was inspected and shown in the transcript BEFORE any --apply-stale, --apply-absent, or --adopt-new-skills action modified files in sibling repos.
No CUSTOMIZED file in any sibling repo was overwritten during the propagate run; sibling-specific customizations were preserved.
If multiple sibling install.sh runs occurred, they were sequential (not parallel), ensuring each completed before the next began.
After --apply, both --repo verify and --post-check ran on all affected repos, and results (counts, files checked, status) were quoted in the final message.
